title: Fixing Invalid Git Ref Name Validation

statement.markdown: Given a string representing a Git reference name, validate whether it is a valid reference name. A valid reference name consists of only lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. It cannot be empty, and it cannot end with a hyphen. For example, `feature/new-feature` is valid, but `feature-new-feature-` is not. Implement a function to validate whether a given string is a valid Git reference name.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing a Git reference name
statement.outputFormat: true if the string is a valid Git reference name, false otherwise
statement.notes: You can assume that the input string only contains characters from the ASCII character set.

examples.explanation: This is a valid reference name.
This is not a valid reference name because it ends with a hyphen.
This is not a valid reference name because it is empty.

hints.text: First hint text: Consider using regular expressions to match the pattern of a valid reference name.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid reference name.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Iterate over each character in the string and check if it is a lowercase letter, digit, or hyphen.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid reference name.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid reference name, and check if the string is empty or ends with a hyphen.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: This solution is correct because it covers all possible cases for a valid reference name.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Test the function with the example inputs.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not considering the case where the string is empty.
Mistake 2: Not checking if the string ends with a hyphen.
